channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A bomb in Gaza's rubble wounds twins who thought it was a toy
item.description: Six-year-old twins in Gaza City were playing outside when they saw a round object and thought it was a toy.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysian foreign minister warned his counterparts from Southeast Asia that the space for neutrality is shrinking in a region increasingly shaped by big power rivalry, as they mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rand Paul: Trump could see 'all hell break loose' with further involvement in Ukraine, Venezuela
item.description: Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) warned on Friday that President Trump's growing involvements in Ukraine's war with Russia and in the Caribbean Sea against Venezuela could further unravel and greatly upset the MAGA base.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU joins US in heaping more sanctions on Russia
item.description: It is a broadened effort to choke off the revenue that funds Moscow’s invasion of Ukraine and to force Putin to negotiate an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: PM Mark Carney says Canada will double its non-US exports
item.description: President Trump has been threatening Canada’s economy and sovereignty with tariffs, most offensively by claiming Canada could be “the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth says US conducted another strike in Eastern Pacific targeting alleged narco-traffickers
item.description: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said on Wednesday that the U.S. military has carried out another strike in the Eastern Pacific targeting alleged narco-terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top UN court says Israel must allow UN relief agency to supply aid to Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Court of Justice said on Wednesday that Israel must allow the U.N. aid agency in Gaza, known as UNRWA, to provide humanitarian assistance to the Palestinian territory. The Hague-based court was asked late last year by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump levies new sanctions on Russian oil giants in a push on Putin to end Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration announced Wednesday new “massive sanctions” against Russia's oil industry that are aimed at moving Russian President Vladimir Putin to the negotiating table and bringing an end to Moscow's brutal war on Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, SUSIE BLANN and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione brought up Brian Thompson's name while in Asia: Acquaintance
item.description: Christian Sacchini noted Luigi Mangione was shocked by how little an MRI scan cost in Thailand as opposed to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Iraq bans Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Iraq has joined several countries — including China, Turkey, Oman and Qatar — to ban U.S. user-generated video game platform Roblox over child safety.
item.dc:creator: Cher Hwang, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre director acknowledges failure after jewel heist and says she offered to resign
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Louvre's director on Wednesday acknowledged a "terrible failure" at the Paris tourist attraction after a daylight crown jewel heist over the weekend, and said that she offered to resign but it was refused.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: American missionary kidnapped in Niger by suspected Islamist militants, sources say
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants have kidnapped an American missionary in the West African nation of Niger, U.S. official sources told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pick for religious freedom envoy visits Holy Land, cites strong US-Israel bond
item.description: Baptist pastor Mark Walker discusses U.S.-Israel spiritual bond and plans to combat global religious persecution after Trump nomination as international religious freedom ambassador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Louvre burglars obtained truck-mounted lift to make off with jewels worth more than $100M
item.description: Thieves used a stolen truck-mounted moving lift to scale the Louvre and steal royal jewels worth over $100 million in a lightning-fast Paris heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles days before Trump's visit to the peninsula
item.description: North Korea test fired ballistic missiles days before President Donald Trump's Asia trip to meet China's Xi Jinping and other leaders in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump doesn’t want ‘wasted meeting’ with Putin as he confirms talks on Ukraine war are off for now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday his plan for a swift meeting with Russian leader Vladimir Putin was on hold because he didn't want it to be a “waste of time.” It was the latest twist in Trump's stop-and-go effort to resolve the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Experts urge Trump to ban terror-linked UN agency from his Gaza peace plan
item.description: Mideast experts urge excluding UNRWA from Gaza reconstruction under President Trump's peace plan, citing the agency's alleged Hamas connections and incompetence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Paris prosecutor says stolen Louvre jewels worth an estimated $102 million
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Paris prosecutor said Tuesday that crown jewels stolen in a dramatic weekend Louvre heist were worth an estimated 88 million euros ($102 million), but that the monetary estimate doesn’t include their historical value to France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan’s first female prime minister, citing Thatcher inspiration
item.description: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan's first female prime minister, citing Margaret Thatcher as inspiration while promising to restore confidence and strengthen security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel confirms how hostage Tal Haimi died, with 15 bodies remaining in Gaza
item.description: Israel identifies the remains of a deceased hostage returned from Gaza as Tal Haimi, who was killed while defending his kibbutz during the Oct. 7 massacre.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dad sends 11-year-old to bed thinking he was drunk; boy later dies of snake bite
item.description: The cause of death for an 11-year-old boy in Australia was confirmed last week by the Coroners Court of Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation

item.title: US envoys visit Israel to bolster truce, and Gaza militants hand over a hostage's remains
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Two of U.S. President Donald Trump’s envoys traveled to Israel on Monday to shore up the ceasefire in Gaza, a day after deadly violence gave the fragile deal its first major test.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy calls Trump meeting 'positive' though he didn't get Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says his reportedly tense meeting with U.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Burqa ban: Portugal may make it illegal to cover face in public
item.description: If signed into law, Portugal would join Austria, France, Belgium and the Netherlands, who have full or partial bans on face and head coverings.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre heist thieves had 'inside help': Former jewel thief
item.description: Lawton described the operation as "very well planned" and explained that before pulling off a heist like this, "you have to know so much.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia recalls US ambassador after Trump's tariff threat, drug remarks
item.description: Trump also called Colombian's leftist President Gustavo Petro an "illegal drug leader" on Sunday, which Petro's government described as offensive.
item.dc:creator: Luis Jaime Acosta and Julia Symmes Cobb / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Pope meets with global group of clergy abuse survivors to talk zero tolerance
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV met Monday for the first time with an organization of clergy abuse survivors and advocates, who said he agreed to maintain a permanent dialogue as they press for a zero-tolerance policy for abuse in the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A cargo aircraft skids off a Hong Kong runway into the sea, killing 2 airport workers
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cargo aircraft skidded off a Hong Kong runway and collided with a security patrol vehicle before both fell into the sea early Monday, killing the two people in the car, authorities said. The plane's four crew members were unhurt.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM and KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel resumes ceasefire in Gaza and says aid deliveries will restart Monday
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Gaza's fragile ceasefire faced its first major test Sunday as Israeli forces launched a wave of deadly strikes, saying Hamas militants had killed two soldiers, and an Israeli security official said the transfer of aid into the territory was halted.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thieves steal crown jewels in 4 minutes from Louvre Museum
item.description: PARIS (AP) — In a minutes-long strike Sunday inside the world’s most-visited museum, thieves rode a basket lift up the Louvre ’s facade, forced a window, smashed display cases and fled with priceless Napoleonic jewels, officials said.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians disappointed after Trump-Zelenskyy meeting fails to immediately secure Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainians shared their disappointment Saturday that the U.S. may not provide Kyiv with long-range Tomahawk missiles, while work to repair the damaged power supply to the country's Zaporizhzhia power plant soothed other concerns surrounding Europe's largest nuclear plant.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
